sanskrit:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः।
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि॥
transliteration: na ca tasmān manuṣyeṣu kaścin me priya-kṛttamaḥ | bhavitā na ca me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi ||
literal_meaning: And not / therefore / among humans / anyone / to Me / more dear-doer / will become / not / and / of Me / than him / other / dearer / on earth.
contextual_meaning: Krishna crowns the person who both studies and gifts this wisdom as the single most beloved human—past, present, or future—because such an act fuses self-actualization with collective healing, dissolving the therapist–client boundary into one field of care.
psychological_state: Secure existential belonging—felt safety in being ‘seen’ by the archetypal Witness while simultaneously invited into radical intimacy.
trigger_scenario: A clinician who, after years of burnout, anonymously funds a trauma-informed yoga program for refugees and hears a client say, ‘For the first time I feel the universe has my back.’
anchor_text: Share wisdom freely; in giving it you become the dearest to the Divine.
